focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Highest scene count, event count, and timeline_worthiness score; central character with extensive relational and status updates indicating deep narrative involvement.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: High task3_readiness due to frequent updates and long-range continuity, making him ideal for character role-playing evaluation.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Steve-Dave receives a check and mocking note from Holden and Banky, compensating for the broken window; Walt wants revenge, but legal action is dismissed.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: focal character's initial reaction to public criticism
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden faces direct mockery from fans at a comic store, who dismiss him and Banky as untalented and their work as low-quality, undermining his sense of artistic value despite the comic's release.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: maintain artistic integrity while tolerating commercial work
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: defensive and disillusioned by public ridicule
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: conflict between artistic self-worth and commercial validation
evidence_quotes: HOLDEN opens the comic and flips through it. He shakes his head.
HOLDEN: I know my name.

relation_updates: strained rapport with Steve-Dave and Walt
status_updates: co-creator of a commercially released comic
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I know my name — but do I matter?

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Banky mocks Holden's 'shared moment' phrase as Alyssa and the girl keep kissing; Holden remains shocked while Banky laughs and slaps his back.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden bonds with Alyssa over shared hometown history and encourages her to perform, reinforcing his emotional involvement.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden believes Alyssa dedicated her song to him, feeling personally chosen, though her actual focus remains unclear.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Alyssa's true romantic interest remains ambiguous.
evidence_quotes: HOLDEN Get out of here! I'm from Highlands!
ALYSSA Another friend - Julie Dwyer. She died in the.. HOLDEN Y.M.C.A pool! Damn! You knew her too!
ALYSSA Alright. I should dedicate this, right? (thinks) This is for that special someone our there.

relation_updates: mutual nostalgia and personal bonding with Alyssa Jones
status_updates: actively supporting Alyssa during her performance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: She dedicated the song to someone special—I thought it was me.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alyssa and Holden discuss intimacy, with Alyssa demonstrating her unusually large tongue, challenging Holden's notions of sex and pleasure, leaving him stunned as she invites him to join her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: engaging in a personal conversation about identity and attraction
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alyssa discusses her sexuality with Holden, challenging his assumptions about gender, attraction, and virginity, leading to a moment of emotional tension and self-reflection for Holden.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understand Alyssa's perspective while reconciling his own beliefs
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden is left unsettled by Alyssa's candid views on sexuality and intimacy, exposing his discomfort with non-normative perspectives
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Holden can move beyond conventional views of sex and relationships
evidence_quotes: HOLDEN (beat) Why girls?
HOLDEN Because that's the standard
ALYSSA Again with the standards.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: She opened her mouth and I saw her tongue—completely unprepared

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holden reveals he is in love with Alyssa, causing Banky to confront his jealousy and prejudice, ultimately leading to a strained silence and their separation.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden tells Banky he is in love with Alyssa, leading to Banky's stunned silence and departure, marking the breakdown of their friendship.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden admits his feelings for Alyssa aloud for the first time, accepting the immediate rupture of his relationship with Banky.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: His feelings for Alyssa are not reciprocated or addressed by her, and the future of their relationship remains uncertain.
evidence_quotes: HOLDEN I'm in love with her, man.
Banky stares at him. Holden stares back. Banky looks into Holden's eyes. Suddenly, he softens a bit. He drops his head.

relation_updates: love for Alyssa Jones declared internally
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I'm in love with her, man.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Hooper critiques male sexual entitlement and the illusion of novelty in relationships, urging honesty about past experiences, while both characters confront societal and personal delusions around sexuality and identity.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: confronting internalized double standards about female sexuality
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden admits that rumors of Alyssa having sex with two men bother him, while acknowledging that similar rumors about her with women do not, revealing his inconsistent reactions based on gender.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally unsettled by his own inconsistent reactions to Alyssa's past
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: struggles to reconcile his progressive self-image with his visceral discomfort about Alyssa's sexual history with men
evidence_quotes: Actually, it's kind of gotten to me.
So what do you care?
Well that's the thing, isn't it? I shouldn't.. but it gets to me.
You see - that doesn't bother me. But the thought of her and guys... Uh!

relation_updates: strained trust with Banky due to perceived betrayal
status_updates: confronts his own double standard about female sexuality
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I shouldn't care, but it gets to me

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holden reflects on a photo of Alyssa in the park, then suddenly gets an idea and rushes off.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden expresses discomfort with Alyssa's sexual history, revealing his struggle to reconcile his feelings for her with intrusive thoughts about her past experiences.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to reconcile love with discomfort over Alyssa's history
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden remains emotionally conflicted, unable to move past vivid mental images of Alyssa's past relationships
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: difficulty accepting Alyssa's sexual history without judgment
evidence_quotes: HOLDEN Right now? (beat) I don't know. I love her. But she has a past.

relation_updates: strained trust with Alyssa due to fixation on her past
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I see kids. I see grand-kids. I can't get the images out of my head

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alyssa leaves after sharing her tragic love story; Hooper and Banky arrive, argue over a comic, notice car damage, and leave with Holden, discussing ambiguous character dynamics.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden recounts parochial school experiences with self-deprecating humor, referencing conflicts with nuns and alcohol use as an altar boy, contributing to a moment of intimacy with Alyssa.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: build rapport through shared humor and personal history
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden reveals discomfort with his religious upbringing through jokes, using humor to navigate personal history
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: his tendency to deflect serious topics with humor
evidence_quotes: Sharp! No. I'm just a fan of clergy-molestation humor. Probably why the extended family quit inviting me to First Communion parties.

status_updates: engaged in private conversation at bar
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I couldn't tell you how many mornings after serious benders I'd wake up next to strange priests

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holden hesitates on the offer, prompting Banky to mock his indecision and defend his own gullibility in buying a fake bootleg, which Sloss questions, while Banky insists the seller looked honest.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden voices opposition to the M-TV cartoon deal, citing concerns about artistic credibility, while Banky and Sloss react with disbelief and mockery.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: preserve artistic credibility of 'Bluntman and Chronic'
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden is isolated as the only one rejecting the cartoon deal, creating immediate tension with Banky and Sloss.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Holden will maintain his stance or yield to pressure to accept the cartoon deal
evidence_quotes: HOLDEN (interrupting) I don't think it's a good idea.
HOLDEN But it'll be all glossy and main- stream. We'll lose any artistic credibility we ever had.
( to Holden) You are out of your fucking mind, aren't you!
BANKY (to Sloss) He just has to get over this crush of his.

auxiliary.persona_anchor: I can't sell out like this — it's not what we made the comic for.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alyssa and Holden discuss intimacy, with Alyssa challenging Holden's views on sex and pleasure; she demonstrates her point by revealing her unusually large tongue, leaving Holden stunned and needing a moment.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: navigating intimacy and sexuality with Alyssa
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden has a direct conversation with Alyssa about attraction and intimacy, during which she challenges his reliance on societal norms to define relationships and sexuality, prompting visible discomfort and reflection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understand Alyssa and his own feelings toward her
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden is unsettled by Alyssa's rejection of conventional definitions of sex and attraction, and begins to question his own assumptions.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Holden can accept non-heteronormative views of intimacy and move beyond rigid definitions of sexuality
evidence_quotes: Do you know how pretty you are?
Holden looks away, embarrassed
Because that's the standard

status_updates: confronted with non-normative views on sex and identity
auxiliary.persona_anchor: She said I'm pretty — not just my looks, but how I see things.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Holden confesses his transformative love to Alyssa, willing to risk their friendship for something deeper; she leaves in silence, leaving him heartbroken and calling after her with lingering hope.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden confesses deep, genuine love to Alyssa and acknowledges the risk to their friendship; she responds by silently leaving the car.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden is left alone, emotionally exposed and heartbroken after Alyssa departs without speaking
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether Alyssa will respond to or acknowledge Holden's confession
evidence_quotes: he confesses, 'I love you.'
Then, without a word, she opens the door and gets out.
He calls after her, 'Was it something I said?'

relation_updates: romantic declaration rejected by Alyssa Jones
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I told her I loved her — really loved her — and she just walked away

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Hooper exposes the illusion of sexual novelty, urging Holden to confront Alyssa's past honestly, while highlighting societal delusions and performative intimacy.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Holden acknowledges his discomfort with rumors about Alyssa's sexual history, particularly involving men, and begins to recognize his own inconsistent reactions based on gender.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: understand his emotional reaction to rumors about Alyssa
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: unsettled and introspective about his double standards regarding female sexuality
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether he can reconcile his attraction to Alyssa with his discomfort over her past sexual experiences with men
evidence_quotes: Banky's not known for believing misinformation. He's got a pretty good bullshit detector.
Well that's the thing, isn't it? I shouldn't.. but it gets to me.
You see - that doesn't bother me. But the thought of her and guys... Uh!

status_updates: exposed to critique of male sexual entitlement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: I shouldn't.. but it gets to me

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Alyssa ends her relationship with Holden, heartbroken over his threesome proposal, reaffirms her love but asserts her boundaries, and leaves him, returning to Banky.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Alyssa ends her relationship with Holden after rejecting his proposal for a threesome, expressing hurt and a need to protect her boundaries.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Holden seeks validation and emotional security through the threesome proposal; Alyssa seeks to preserve her self-respect and emotional integrity.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Holden is left emotionally shattered and alone after Alyssa ends the relationship and leaves the studio.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Holden struggles to understand love without control or conditions, facing the consequences of his possessive insecurity.
evidence_quotes: Regardless I can't be a part of this. (beat) Or you. Not anymore
I love you. I always will. Know that.
But I'm not your fucking whore.
He's your's again.

relation_updates: Holden and Alyssa's romantic relationship ends
Holden and Banky remain connected but emotionally distant
status_updates: Holden is rejected and emotionally isolated
auxiliary.persona_anchor: She said she loved me — then called me a whore and walked away.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: The timeline traces 12 key developments across the character's screenplay trajectory.
